title: La Chine veut protéger le travail des gens
gist: China uses AI and robots more than almost anywhere, and driverless taxis in the city of Wuhan have already sparked protests from drivers losing work. Fearing public anger — especially now that AI also threatens university graduates — the government is pushing to keep people employed: courts have sided with fired workers, and the state wants AI to help people rather than replace them. It's a state-driven vision, unlike the company-led approach in the United States.

body.fr: En Chine, il y a beaucoup de {{robots|robots}}.

bd.meaning: In China, there are many robots.
chunks: there is / there are — a fixed phrase
many robots (beaucoup de = a lot of)
bd.key: il y a = 'there is / are'. One set phrase covers both: il y a beaucoup de robots = 'there are many robots'.

bd.meaning: The robots work in the factories.
chunks: the robots work — the -ent ending marks the plural subject
bd.key: -ent = a plural subject. robots is plural, so travaillent ends in silent -ent (it sounds like travaille).

body.fr: Il y a aussi des taxis {{sans chauffeur|driverless / without a driver}}.

bd.key: sans = 'without'. A handy little word: sans chauffeur = 'without a driver' — and you don't add a word for 'a'.

body.fr: Dans la ville de Wuhan, les {{chauffeurs de taxi|taxi drivers}} ne sont pas contents.

bd.meaning: In the city of Wuhan, the taxi drivers are not happy.
chunks: are not happy — ne…pas wraps sont; contents takes -s (plural)
bd.key: Negation sandwich: ne … pas. It wraps the verb: ne sont pas = 'are not'.
leur = 'their'. A possessive little word: leur travail = 'their work'.

chunks: is afraid (avoir peur = to be afraid, literally 'has fear')
bd.key: avoir peur = 'to be afraid'. French uses a peur ('has fear') where English uses 'is afraid'.

body.fr: L'{{intelligence artificielle (l'IA)|artificial intelligence (AI)}} peut prendre le travail des gens.

bd.meaning: Artificial intelligence can take people's jobs.
chunks: artificial intelligence — the adjective comes AFTER the noun
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. peut (can) carries the subject; prendre stays plain.

chunks: anger (de la = 'some', before a feminine word)
bd.key: de la = 'some' (feminine). Before colère (a feminine word) French adds de la where English says nothing.

body.fr: Avant, l'IA a surtout {{touché|affected / hit}} les {{ouvriers|blue-collar workers}}.

bd.meaning: Before, AI mainly affected factory workers.
chunks: AI mainly affected (a touché = past of toucher)
bd.key: Past = avoir + verb. a touché = 'affected': a (avoir) + the past form touché.

body.fr: Maintenant, l'IA touche aussi les {{jeunes diplômés|young graduates}}.

bd.meaning: Now, AI also affects young graduates.
chunks: young graduates — jeune is one of the few adjectives placed BEFORE the noun
bd.key: Present tense for 'now'. touche ('affects') — the plain present is the simplest way to say what happens now, against the past a touché just before.

body.fr: La Chine {{veut|wants}} être le numéro un de l'IA.

bd.meaning: China wants to be number one in AI.
chunks: to be number one — être stays plain after veut
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. veut (wants) changes with the subject; the next verb être stays plain.

body.fr: Mais la Chine veut aussi {{garder|keep}} les gens au travail.

bd.meaning: But China also wants to keep people in work.
bd.key: au = à + le. The two words squash together: à + le travail → au travail.

body.fr: Dans son {{plan|plan}}, le gouvernement {{promet|promises}} d'aider les {{travailleurs|workers}}.

bd.meaning: In its plan, the government promises to help workers.
chunks: to help the workers (promettre de + plain verb; de → d')
bd.key: promettre de + plain verb. Some verbs link to the next one with de: promet d'aider = 'promises to help'.

body.fr: Pour la Chine, l'IA {{doit|must}} aider les gens, pas les {{remplacer|replace them}}.

bd.meaning: For China, AI must help people, not replace them.
chunks: not replace them (les = 'them', sits before the verb)
bd.key: devoir = 'must'. doit carries the subject and the next verb aider stays plain — your pouvoir / vouloir / devoir helper family.

body.fr: Une {{entreprise|company}} a {{remplacé|replaced}} un homme par une IA.

bd.meaning: A company replaced a man with an AI.
chunks: replaced a man (a remplacé = past of remplacer)
bd.key: Past = avoir + verb. a remplacé = 'replaced': a (avoir) + the past form remplacé.

chunks: a court said (a dit = past of dire)
it's illegal (c'est = the set phrase 'it is')
bd.key: c'est = 'it is / that's'. The everyday set phrase: c'est illégal = 'it's illegal'.

chunks: different — takes -es to match visions (feminine plural)
bd.key: Adjective agrees with its noun. visions is feminine plural, so différentes adds -es.

bd.meaning: In the United States, the companies decide.
chunks: in the United States (aux = à + les)
the companies decide — -ent marks the plural subject
bd.key: aux = à + les. Like au, but plural: à + les États-Unis → aux États-Unis.

body.fr: En Chine, c'est le gouvernement qui décide.

bd.meaning: In China, it's the government that decides.
chunks: it's the government that decides (c'est … qui = 'it's … that')
bd.key: c'est … qui = 'it's … that'. A frame to spotlight who does something: c'est le gouvernement qui décide.

grammar.fr: l'IA a touché / a remplacé / a dit
grammar.en: avoir + past participle = the past (passé composé). a (from avoir) plus a past form: a touché, a remplacé, a dit. Your 'past = avoir/être + verb' key.

grammar.fr: l'IA peut prendre / veut être / doit aider
grammar.en: helper + plain verb. peut, veut, doit carry the subject; the second verb stays plain. Your pouvoir / vouloir / devoir family.

grammar.fr: au travail / aux États-Unis / des gens
grammar.en: the squashed little words. à + le → au, à + les → aux, de + les → des. They merge automatically — you can't keep them apart.

quiz.q: Pourquoi le gouvernement chinois a peur de l'IA ?
quiz.a: Parce que l'IA peut prendre le travail des gens et créer de la colère. (Because AI can take people's jobs and create anger.)

flag: Compressed hard from a longer analysis piece: I dropped 'A.I. Marxism', the five-year-plan detail, the Brookings expert, and the specific ministries and insurance proposals, keeping the core story. It's a neutral retelling of your article, not a view on China. Two phrasing notes: garder les gens au travail ('keep people in work') reads fine but is my own simplification of 'keeping humans at the core of the economy'; and l'IA touche les ouvriers uses toucher ('to affect / hit') in the ordinary news sense — natural, though a native might also say l'IA menace les emplois ('threatens jobs').
